commands.review_summary:
The PR preserves raw WebChat message text through normal, queued, steered, and controller `chat.send` paths while keeping emptiness, command matching, dedup, and history based on trimmed text, with a focused regression test.

PR surface: Source +15, Tests +23. Total +38 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. The linked issue gives a concrete WebChat code-block repro, and current main source trims composer/controller text before `chat.send`; I did not run the browser flow in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No automated repair is indicated; the remaining blocker is current-head real behavior proof or a maintainer rerun before merge.

target.title: fix(webchat): preserve code block whitespace
target.body:
Fixes #81339

## Summary
- stop trimming normal WebChat send payloads before `chat.send`
- keep slash/local command matching on trimmed text while preserving user-authored chat text
- preserve queued and steered queued message whitespace for code blocks and ASCII diagrams

## Real behavior proof

> Rebase note (head `21125cfb1c`): `main` refactored the Control UI composer pipeline since the original proof below, so this PR was re-implemented onto current `main` (the old diff no longer applied). The user-visible contract is unchanged. The whitespace-preservation behavior is now locked by a regression test asserting the emitted `chat.send` payload keeps leading/trailing whitespace: `ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts` -> "preserves user-authored whitespace (code blocks / ASCII) in chat.send". The headless-Chrome run below is from the original head and documents the same end-to-end behavior; it has not been re-executed against the new head this pass (see "What was not tested").

Behavior addressed: WebChat should send fenced code blocks exactly as typed so leading spaces in ASCII diagrams survive.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw gateway from PR head `a9142c5abb` on macOS, Control UI rebuilt by `pnpm ui:build`, temporary `OPENCLAW_HOME`, token auth on `127.0.0.1:18896`, and headless Chrome exercising the served `/chat` UI. For the local browser run only, `gateway.controlUi.dangerouslyDisableDeviceAuth=true` was set so Chrome could connect over local HTTP without a pairing ceremony; the gateway still used token auth.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
pnpm ui:build
COREPACK_HOME=/Users/betala/.cache/node/corepack HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-proof-81339-home OPENCLAW_HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-proof-81339-home/.openclaw OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/private/tmp/openclaw-proof-81339-home/.openclaw OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/private/tmp/openclaw-proof-81339-home/.openclaw/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=proof-token-81339 OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 node scripts/run-node.mjs --dev gateway --port 18896 --bind custom --allow-unconfigured --verbose --cli-backend-logs
curl -sS -i http://127.0.0.1:18896/healthz
node /private/tmp/openclaw-81339-ui-proof.mjs
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8
Cache-Control: no-store

{"ok":true,"status":"live"}
```

```json
{
  "url": "http://127.0.0.1:18896/chat?session=main",
  "sentMessage": "```text\n+5V --+-- R68 3.3K --+-- TP175\n      |               +-- R122 10K\n      |               +-- Z3 -> GND\n```",
  "preservedExactWhitespace": true,
  "leadingSpacesLine3": "      |               +-- R122 10K",
  "frameMethod": "chat.send",
  "frameSessionKey": "agent:main:main",
  "sawUserBubble": true,
  "pageErrors": []
}
```

Observed result after fix: the running Control UI submitted a `chat.send` websocket frame whose `message` exactly matched the typed fenced code block, including the six leading spaces before the ASCII connector lines.

What was not tested: I did not run a live LLM provider response because the bug is in the Control UI send path before model execution, and the proof intentionally used an unconfigured local gateway. The headless-Chrome browser proof above was not re-executed against the rebased head `21125cfb1c` this pass; the behavior is covered by the new `chat.send`-payload regression test, and the unchanged browser recipe above remains available to re-confirm end-to-end on request.

## Verification (rebased head `21125cfb1c`)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts` -> 78 passed (incl. new whitespace regression test)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts` -> 91 passed
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ui/src/ui/app-chat.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts` -> clean
- `oxfmt --check` on the three files -> clean
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.ui.json` -> clean (exit 0)
- `git diff --check` -> clean
